reasoning: The proposal to build a new arena for the Washington Wizards and Capitals in Alexandria, Virginia officially died in March 2024. Following the collapse of the Virginia deal, Monumental Sports & Entertainment reached an agreement with the District of Columbia to remain at the Capital One Arena until 2050, which included a $515 million commitment to renovate the existing facility. In December 2024, the D.C. Council approved legislation for the city to purchase and renovate the Capital One arena, finalizing the agreement. Because the teams are legally and financially committed to D.C. for decades and there is no active proposal, design, or legislative effort taking place in Virginia, it is virtually impossible for an arena for these teams to break ground in Northern Virginia by January 1, 2026.
